introText: If you work in Fresno and have been wrongfully terminated, discriminated against, harassed, or denied fair wages, The Azizi Firm is here to help. As a California employment law firm, we represent workers throughout Fresno County against employers of all sizes - from agricultural processing plants and healthcare systems to logistics operations and educational institutions.
localInfo: Fresno is the economic hub of the Central Valley, with major employers in agriculture, food processing, healthcare, logistics, and education. The EEOC Fresno Local Office and California DLSE Fresno field office handle employment claims from Central Valley workers. The Fresno County Superior Court at the B.F. Sisk Courthouse hears employment law cases including wrongful termination, discrimination, harassment, and wage disputes involving agricultural and industrial workers.

sections.header: Employment Law Cases We Handle in Fresno
sections.body: Our Fresno employment attorneys handle: wrongful termination, workplace discrimination (race, gender, age, disability, sexual orientation), sexual harassment, hostile work environment, whistleblower retaliation, wage and hour violations (unpaid wages, overtime, meal/rest breaks), employee misclassification, FMLA/CFRA violations, and employment contract disputes. We particularly focus on protecting agricultural and food processing workers in the Central Valley.

sections.header: California Employment Laws That Protect Fresno Workers
sections.body: The California Fair Employment and Housing Act (FEHA) provides stronger protections than federal law. California Labor Code sections 1102.5, 98.6, and 6310 protect whistleblowers. The California Family Rights Act (CFRA) provides job-protected leave. Agricultural workers have additional protections under the California Labor Code, including overtime pay requirements and heat illness prevention standards.

faq.question: How long do I have to file an employment claim in California?
faq.answer: Statute of limitations vary by claim type. For FEHA discrimination claims, you must first file with the California Civil Rights Department (CRD) within three years. For wrongful termination claims, you generally have two years. For wage claims, you generally have three years. Contact us immediately to ensure your claim is timely filed.

faq.question: What compensation can I recover in an employment law case?
faq.answer: You may be entitled to lost wages (back pay and front pay), emotional distress damages, punitive damages, attorney fees, reinstatement, and other remedies. The value depends on your specific situation - call us for a free evaluation.

whyHireText: We understand California employment law and have experience litigating against large employers. Our firm's founder previously defended major insurance companies, giving us insider knowledge of how defendants approach employment cases. We leverage this experience to fight for maximum compensation for our clients.
contactUsText: If you're facing a workplace issue in Fresno, contact The Azizi Firm today for a free, confidential consultation. We serve employees throughout Fresno County and all of California.
